content:
Hi, I am a final year computer science student studying at Boston University. I am from Bangalore, India. My interests are in operating systems, embedded and real-time oses. I also like to tinker with hardware and build stuff.
 Education Some of my relevant coursework include:
 CS210: Introduction to Computer Systems  CS320: Introduction to Programming Languages CS330: Introduction to Algorithms  CS350: Computer System Fundamentals  CS410: Advanced Software Systems  CS525: Introduction to Compiler Design  EC535: Introduction to Embedded Systems EC527: Multi-core GPU Programming  CS350: Introduction to Operating Systems (Also known as CS552)  In addition to the courses I have taken at Boston University, I am also a certified from XRTerra as an AR/VR developer through their AR x VR Developer Program .
Experience BU Spark! X-Lab Project Intern  Researching the feasibility of running a drone flight controller software on an x86 platform.  Boston University Rocket Propulsion Group Software Developer  Designed and developed a data acquisition platform for a rocket engine test stand test stand. Researched into building a real-time embedded flight computer. Maintained the entire tech stack for internal team communication.  Simplify360 Natural Language Processing Intern  Researched and developed an automated text tagging and classification based on the clients' requirements to interface with their B2B systems.  Remidio Innovative Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Data Science Intern  Leveraged their tele-medicine platform to develop and train a model to automatically grade fundus images for the presence of age related macular degeneration. The model achieved 87% accuracy in validation.  Spectral Insights Computer Vision Intern  Developed a pipeline module that provided doctors with analytics on kidney biopsies.   Contact The best way to reach me is by email .

Hi, I am a final year computer science student studying at Boston University. I am from Bangalore, India. My interests are in operating systems, embedded and real-time oses. I also like to tinker with hardware and build stuff.
 Education Some of my relevant coursework include:
 CS210: Introduction to Computer Systems  CS320: Introduction to Programming Languages CS330: Introduction to Algorithms  CS350: Computer System Fundamentals  CS410: Advanced Software Systems  CS525: Introduction to Compiler Design  EC535: Introduction to Embedded Systems EC527: Multi-core GPU Programming  CS350: Introduction to Operating Systems (Also known as CS552)  In addition to the courses I have taken at Boston University, I am also a certified from XRTerra as an AR/VR developer through their AR x VR Developer Program .
title: About Me

content:
How it works Hosting this site is pretty straightforward. I use hugo to generate the website from a list of markdown files. Read more about hugo here .
There are many tutorials out there that will teach you how to setup a new hugo project, so I won’t go into the details of starting a new hugo project.
Getting started But the gist of it is:
  Run hugo new site <path to site>.
  Edit the config.[toml|yml|json] file in the newly generated directory.
  Set a theme in the config file.
  Run hugo serve to launch a development server to locally preview the new site.
  Installing a new theme There are multiple ways to install a new theme into the new project we created. My preferred way is to add it as a git sub-module in the themes/ subdirectory as I use git to maintain my project however any of the ways mentioned below will work.
  Use git: git submodule add <url to the repo> --depth=1 themes/.
  Download the theme and extract it to the theme folder.
  Modifying the theme Hugo has powerful templating engine that is used to generate the site from the markdown files.
summary:

title: Hugo Website
